articles.title: Senate confirms Markwayne Mullin to lead Homeland Security as TSA standoff deepens
articles.description:
Mullin, a Republican senator from Oklahoma known for his close friendship with Trump, has tried to present himself as a steady hand, saying his goal as secretary would be to get the department off the front page of the news.

The post Senate confirms Markwayne Mullin to lead Homeland Security as TSA standoff deepens appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Senate confirms Markwayne Mullin as Homeland Security Secretary on March 23, 2026.
Mullin aims to stabilize TSA amid ongoing operational challenges affecting travelers.
Boston residents may experience improved airport security and reduced travel disruptions.

articles.title: State House Minority Leader opts not to run again after 32 years in office
articles.description:
“Serving as State Representative has been a tremendously meaningful part of my life, and while difficult to leave behind, I eagerly look forward to my next chapter,” the representative said.

The post State House Minority Leader opts not to run again after 32 years in office appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: State House Minority Leader opts not to seek re-election after 32 years in office.
Representative expressed gratitude for his tenure, emphasizing its significance in his life.
His departure may open opportunities for new leadership in Boston's legislative landscape.

articles.title: No ICE agents at Logan Airport yet as agents deploy to busy airports
articles.description:
ICE agents were not deployed to any New England airport, the TSA union president told Boston.com, but could come next week.

The post No ICE agents at Logan Airport yet as agents deploy to busy airports appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: ICE agents have not yet been deployed to Logan Airport as of now.
TSA union president indicated agents could arrive next week.
Boston residents may face increased scrutiny at airports if ICE agents deploy.

articles.title: Fitchburg fire chief on paid leave pending external investigation, mayor says
articles.description:
Fitchburg Fire Chief Dante Suarez has been on paid leave since last month. The city declined to discuss details.

The post Fitchburg fire chief on paid leave pending external investigation, mayor says appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Fitchburg Fire Chief Dante Suarez has been on paid leave since February 2023.
The city has not disclosed details regarding the investigation into Chief Suarez.
Residents may experience changes in fire department leadership during the investigation period.

articles.title: Person seriously injured in Seaport stabbing
articles.description:
A man was stabbed in his abdomen and leg, police said.

The post Person seriously injured in Seaport stabbing appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: A man sustained serious injuries from a stabbing in the Seaport area.
Police reported the victim was stabbed in the abdomen and leg.
This incident raises safety concerns for Boston residents in the Seaport district.

articles.title: Here’s what happened during the first day of Kelsey Fitzsimmons’s trial
articles.description:
Fitzsimmons was shot by a colleague last year. Fitzsimmons is charged with assault and battery with a dangerous weapon. A bench trial is ongoing.

The post Here’s what happened during the first day of Kelsey Fitzsimmons’s trial appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Kelsey Fitzsimmons's trial began on March 23, 2026, focusing on her shooting incident.
Fitzsimmons faces charges of assault and battery with a dangerous weapon.
The outcome may influence workplace safety policies for Boston employees.
